briefing.headline: Rig up a Ned Rig — aggressive fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Ned Rig is a strong choice for fall with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: In clear water, they'll bite the Ned Rig on the fall. Cast out, let it sink on dead slack line — don't tighten up until it hits bottom.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: In clear water, they'll bite the Ned Rig on the fall. Cast out, let it sink on dead slack line — don't tighten up until it hits bottom.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Robertson: Use a Ned rig with a 1/4-ounce mushroom-style jighead and green-pumpkin Ned Ocho on spinning gear (10-pound braid to 6-pound fluorocarbon leader) to target smallmouth holding on boulders in
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Johnston: Fall smallmouth fishing is all about location and timing. Cover water fast with reaction baits to find active groups, then slow down with finesse presentations to catch the rest. If fish stop biting a tube, switch to Ned rig or drop shot.
Wheeler: In fall, fish school up in ditches, on little flats in 2-4 feet, and especially around grass; on grassless lakes, key on shallow ditches with stumps that let bass ambush shad.

KVD: 'Biggest smallmouth cranking difference: it's NOT about bottom contact. Keep crankbait a few feet off bottom — smallmouth in clear water love to feed looking up. It's about speed and covering water, casting all directions to find roaming aggr...
Robertson: In fall, smallmouth stack up on boulder fields in 20 feet of water where baitfish are concentrated. The fish can be found within a very small 60-foot area when they're grouped up.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal fall bass are following shad into the backs of creeks. Fish can be anywhere from 3-18ft depending on bait position. Target creek arms, secondary points, and anywhere baitfish are concentrating.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
